Class ReaderDaoImpl

java.lang.Object
com.library.dao.impl.ReaderDaoImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
ReaderDao
public class ReaderDaoImpl extends Object implements ReaderDao
JDBC implementation of ReaderDao.

All queries are executed via PreparedStatement — no string concatenation is used anywhere in this class.

The search(String, LocalDate, LocalDate) method builds a dynamic WHERE clause at runtime from the provided filter arguments.

SQL state 23503 (foreign-key violation) from PostgreSQL is caught in delete(int) and re-thrown as a descriptive RuntimeException so the controller layer can display a user-friendly error dialog.

      private static final String FK_VIOLATION

      public ReaderDaoImpl()

  • Method Details

    • conn

      private Connection conn()
      Returns:
      the active JDBC connection from the singleton pool

    • mapRow

      private Reader mapRow(ResultSet rs) throws SQLException
      Maps the current row of a ResultSet to a Reader object.

      If reg_date is NULL in the database (should not happen given the schema default), falls back to LocalDate.now().

      Parameters:
      rs - the result set positioned on the row to map
      Returns:
      a fully populated Reader
      Throws:
      SQLException - if any column access fails

    • findAll

      public List<Reader> findAll()
      Description copied from interface: ReaderDao
      Returns all readers ordered by full_name ascending.
      Specified by:
      findAll in interface ReaderDao
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable snapshot; never null

    • findById

      public Optional<Reader> findById(int id)
      Description copied from interface: ReaderDao
      Finds a single reader by its primary key.
      Specified by:
      findById in interface ReaderDao
      Parameters:
      id - the reader ID to look up
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the reader, or empty if not found

    • search

      public List<Reader> search(String text, LocalDate regFrom, LocalDate regTo)
      Builds and executes a dynamic WHERE clause.

      The text term is matched against full_name, email, and phone using COALESCE to safely handle NULL values in nullable columns. Dynamic multi-field search with optional date-range filter.

      • text — case-insensitive LIKE match against full_name, email, and phone; ignored if blank
      • regFrom — reg_date >= regFrom; ignored if null
      • regTo — reg_date <= regTo; ignored if null
      Specified by:
      search in interface ReaderDao
      Parameters:
      text - free-text search term; may be blank
      regFrom - lower bound for registration date; may be null
      regTo - upper bound for registration date; may be null
      Returns:
      matching readers ordered by full_name; never null

    • insert

      public void insert(Reader r)
      Description copied from interface: ReaderDao
      Inserts a new reader record. The id field is ignored; the generated key is assigned by the database.
      Specified by:
      insert in interface ReaderDao
      Parameters:
      r - the reader to insert (fullName must be set)

    • update

      public void update(Reader r)
      Description copied from interface: ReaderDao
      Updates an existing reader record identified by reader.getId().
      Specified by:
      update in interface ReaderDao
      Parameters:
      r - the reader with updated fields; id must be valid

    • delete

      public void delete(int id)
      Deletes the reader with the given id.

      If the reader still has loan records, PostgreSQL raises SQL state 23503. This is caught and re-thrown as a RuntimeException with a human-readable message so the controller can show an error dialog instead of silently failing.

      Specified by:
      delete in interface ReaderDao
      Parameters:
      id - the primary key of the reader to delete

    • query

      private List<Reader> query(String sql, List<Object> args)
      Executes a parameterized SELECT and maps all rows to Reader objects.
      Parameters:
      sql - the fully formed SQL query string
      args - ordered list of bind values (String, Date, or LocalDate)
      Returns:
      list of matched readers; never null

    • setNullableString

      private void setNullableString(PreparedStatement ps, int i, String v) throws SQLException
      Binds a String parameter or sets SQL NULL when blank.
      Parameters:
      ps - the prepared statement
      i - 1-based parameter index
      v - string value; null or blank → SQL NULL
      Throws:
      SQLException
